name: Jeff's Favorite Barbecue Brisket
recipeInstructions:
This is a perfect way to spend a day, and makes a meal fit for a king!
It really takes a smoker to make this successfully, but creative folks can use whatever's handy.
Select a good quality beef brisket. Trim off excess fat and store in refrigerator until ready to barbecue.
If desired, marinade the meat for additional flavor or tenderness using water, Worcestershire Sauce, Soy Sauce, pepper, lime or lemon juice (no salt).
Start with 8 lbs of charcoal and allow them to develop gray ash over all before beginning to cook. Reserve the remaining 2 lbs for adding later as needed.
Add wood that has been soaking in water for an hour or so.
Cook the meat on a grate approximately 8 inches over the coals, adding charcoal and wood to the fire as needed. It isn't necessary to maintain heavy smoke throughout; just half of the cooking time is sufficient. Use a meat thermometer to determine doneness according to preference. If the smoker has an indicator for temperature, low heat is fine.
While cooking, baste often with the basting sauce every 10 minutes or less to ensure a juicy and delicious brisket.
If using another sauce for basting, remember that anything containing sugar or tomato will burn, leaving a scorched flavor, especially when meats are cooked over long periods.
Serve with warmed barbecue sauce and accompaniments such as breads, beans, salad, or potatoes.
